our_argument_summary: Our review says the 11-12 Hz region is a Schumann inter-harmonic minimum (standard spherical-cavity physics), that the first harmonic at 14.3 Hz directly falsifies any 'cutoff,' and that the dome's own code hardcodes this as a static win. Cites Chave & Jones 2012. Verdict: Refuted by Data.

cycle_2_delta.changes_applied: All three Cycle 1 issues were addressed: (1) Wrong DOI replaced with Chave & Jones 2012, (2) Dead band conflation replaced with precise inter-harmonic minimum language, (3) 14.3 Hz harmonic argument now leads the rebuttal. Code analysis 'static win' detail also added.
cycle_2_delta.improvement_assessment: Substantial. The evidence text was largely rewritten to incorporate all Cycle 1 recommendations. The argument is now significantly stronger and more precise.

relevant_files: monitor.py domain #25 'Telluric Resonance Cutoff' (referenced from Cycle 1 analysis; monitor.py not present in raw-text/ directory)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Completely static. Domain #25 hardcodes predicted=11.7, observed=11.7, error_pct=0.0, pass=True. No MT data is fetched. Source field self-labels as '(WIN-008 static win)'.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Everything hardcoded. Both prediction and observation are static strings. No computation occurs.
data_sources_used: None. Source field claims 'IRIS / Geometrics MT data' but no API call is made.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None needed — always passes by construction.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None remaining. The Cycle 1 recommendation to mention the 'static win' self-label was implemented.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Domain #25 in monitor.py hardcodes both predicted and observed values as 11.7 Hz with 0.0% error and pass=True. No IRIS or Geometrics API call. The source field itself labels this '(WIN-008 static win)'. Confirmed in Cycle 1; no dome repo changes reported since.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: The inter-harmonic minimum between ~7.83 Hz and ~14.3 Hz is a standard feature of spherical Earth-ionosphere cavity resonance physics, documented since Schumann (1952) and Balser & Wagner (1960). The dome relabels this as a 'disc resonance ceiling' without changing any numerical prediction.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Schumann resonances and their inter-harmonic power structure have been measured since 1960 (Balser & Wagner). MT spectral characteristics including the 11-12 Hz region have been documented for decades. The dome adopted these known values as 'predictions' after the fact.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The dome's disc resonance formula f=c/(2D) does produce ~11.78 Hz from dome geometry (disc thickness). However, the specific claim of a spectral 'cutoff' at 11.7 Hz is not derived from any dome equation — the dome doesn't derive that there should be a spectral cutoff at its resonance frequency. It observes a power minimum near 11.7 Hz in MT data and notes the frequency coincidence. The predicted frequency has a dome derivation path, but the phenomenon (cutoff/minimum) does not. Marking false is defensible but borderline — see holes_found for discussion.

holes_found.description: DERIVES_FROM_DOME TAG AMBIGUITY: The current tag is false, but the dome does derive ~11.78 Hz from f=c/(2D) using disc thickness. The predicted FREQUENCY has a dome derivation path. What lacks dome derivation is the PHENOMENON — the dome doesn't derive from its geometry that there should be a spectral cutoff at its resonance frequency. Current tag of false is defensible because the tag asks about the predicted value in context (i.e., 'a cutoff at 11.7 Hz'), not just the bare frequency. But a dome defender could argue this is one of the few WINs where the number actually comes from dome parameters.
holes_found.recommendation: Consider adding a note to the code_analysis documentation that derives_from_dome evaluates whether the full claim (phenomenon + value) is derived, not just the numerical coincidence. Alternatively, change to true with a note that the derivation produces a resonance frequency, not a cutoff prediction. Either position is defensible — just be consistent with how WIN-001 (same formula) is tagged.

holes_found.description: MONITOR.PY NOT IN RAW-TEXT: Our evidence text references 'Domain #25 in monitor.py' but monitor.py is not captured in the raw-text/ directory. This reference relies on analysis from Cycle 1 and earlier agents. If the dome updates monitor.py, we can't verify our claims against the current version without re-fetching.
holes_found.recommendation: Low priority. The dome poller would detect site changes. But if possible, capture monitor.py in raw-text/ for auditability.

holes_found.description: CHAVE & JONES CITATION PRECISION: The text cites 'Chave & Jones, 2012, The Magnetotelluric Method' for the claim that the inter-harmonic minimum is a 'standard feature of the spherical Earth-ionosphere cavity resonance spectrum.' While Chave & Jones does discuss natural source fields including Schumann resonances, a more direct citation for the Schumann harmonic structure would be Nickolaenko & Hayakawa (2002) 'Resonances in the Earth-Ionosphere Cavity' (Springer) or Sentman (1995). The current citation is adequate but not optimal — a dome defender with access to the book could argue it's primarily about MT methodology, not cavity resonance physics.
holes_found.recommendation: No change required. Chave & Jones is a legitimate and accessible reference that discusses natural source spectra. Adding a second reference like Nickolaenko & Hayakawa would strengthen the citation but is not necessary.

stronger_arguments.description: WIN-008/WIN-001 CIRCULAR DEPENDENCY: WIN-001 claims f=c/(2D)=11.78 Hz as a confirmed resonance, and WIN-008 claims the same frequency appears as a 'cutoff' in telluric data. These are the same underlying claim counted twice — the dome uses one formula to generate two WINs. Our review already groups them (detail_group: WIN-008/009) but could explicitly note this double-counting in the detail text.

kernel_of_truth.description: There is genuinely lower spectral power density in the 11-12 Hz range of the natural ELF electromagnetic spectrum. This is a real, measurable feature — the inter-harmonic minimum between the Schumann fundamental (~7.83 Hz) and first overtone (~14.3 Hz). The dome model correctly identifies that this frequency region has less electromagnetic energy than the adjacent Schumann peaks.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The inter-harmonic minimum is a standard prediction of spherical Earth-ionosphere cavity resonance physics, fully explained since Schumann (1952). More critically, the claim of a 'sharp cutoff' is directly falsified by the robust first harmonic at 14.3 Hz — if there were a resonance ceiling at 11.7 Hz, no cavity modes could exist above it. The dome relabels a well-understood spectral feature as evidence for its own geometry while ignoring the data that contradicts the specific 'cutoff' claim.

deeper_analysis:
Cycle 2 review of WIN-008 finds the text in substantially improved condition compared to Cycle 1. All three issues identified in Cycle 1 were addressed: the wrong DOI (10.1007/978-3-540-71031-4, which resolved to an unrelated elasticity book) was replaced with Chave & Jones (2012); the conflation of the MT dead band (800 Hz-5 kHz) with the Schumann inter-harmonic minimum was corrected; and the stronger 14.3 Hz harmonic argument now leads the rebuttal.

The current argument structure is clean and effective: (1) the 11-12 Hz region is an inter-harmonic minimum in standard spherical-cavity physics, (2) the first Schumann harmonic at 14.3 Hz directly falsifies any 'cutoff' at 11.7 Hz, (3) the dome's own code admits this is a static assertion. This three-pronged approach covers factual rebuttal, standard physics explanation, and code analysis.

The verdict of 'Refuted by Data' remains correct. The specific claim of a 'sharp cutoff' is directly falsified by measured harmonic data — this is stronger than 'Std Model Explains' because the dome makes a falsifiable claim (no energy above 11.7 Hz) that is falsified by observation (energy at 14.3, 20.8, 27.3, 33.8 Hz).

The only substantive issue carried forward is the derives_from_dome tag, which is false but could be argued either way. The dome does derive ~11.78 Hz from f=c/(2D), but the 'cutoff' phenomenon is not derived from dome geometry. This is a tagging ambiguity, not an error in our published text.

Code analysis tags are confirmed accurate. No new external evidence or dome site changes affect this WIN.

citations_verified: Chave & Jones, 2012, The Magnetotelluric Method — correct title, authors, year, publisher (Cambridge UP). Discusses natural source fields including Schumann resonances as part of MT methodology.
citations_unchecked: monitor.py domain #25 reference — monitor.py not in raw-text/ directory, relying on Cycle 1 verification
